title: saturating_add in select_notes_spendable inconsistent with plain .sum() in caller
description: In select_notes_spendable, sum is accumulated with sum.saturating_add(note.value_zatoshi). In build_shielded_tx, the caller recomputes: let selected_sum: u64 = selected_notes.iter().map(|n| n.value_zatoshi).sum(). If selected notes have values summing to > u64::MAX (impossible with real ZEC supply ~2.1e15 zatoshi << u64::MAX ~1.84e19, but possible with corrupted DB), saturating_add caps at u64::MAX making sum >= total_needed true. The caller's .sum() then panics in debug or wraps in release, producing wrong change. The two paths have different overflow semantics with no comment explaining why saturating_add was chosen or why .sum() is safe. A contributor who sees saturating_add and tries to add a checked sum in the caller will be confused. The inconsistency also makes the safety argument harder to audit — if the 'impossible in practice' assumption ever breaks, the two diverge silently in release.
